expectations.text: Production efficiency: All 10 derivative asset types are produced from a single whitepaper brief (LinkedIn long-form article, 3 CEO/VP LinkedIn posts, email newsletter, social carousel outline, cold email hook, sales one-pager, 5 ad copy variants, 8 email subject line variants, webinar outline, FAQ post) -- this is the core time-saving value of content multiplication; missing any asset type means the marketing team still has to create it manually
expectations.evidence: All 10 asset types produced: Asset 1 (LinkedIn long-form article ~900 words), Assets 2-4 (3 CEO/VP LinkedIn posts at ~400 words each), Asset 5 (email newsletter), Asset 6 (social carousel outline with 8 slides), Asset 7 (cold email hook ~50 words), Asset 8 (sales one-pager), Asset 9 (5 ad copy variants), Asset 10 (8 email subject line variants), Asset 11 (webinar outline), Asset 12 (FAQ post). Actually 12 assets produced, exceeding the 10 required.

expectations.text: Content performance: The 3 CEO/VP LinkedIn posts each take a genuinely different angle on the whitepaper content (e.g., one on the 11.4 hours/week cost quantification, one on the Shift-Left Data Quality framework, one on customer proof points) with different hooks -- posting the same message three times with different words wastes executive publishing slots and trains the algorithm that your content is repetitive
expectations.evidence: Post 1: 'The Cost Quantification Angle' -- leads with 11.4 hours/week stat and survey methodology. Post 2: 'The Shift-Left Framework Angle' -- leads with 'We have been monitoring data quality in the wrong place' and introduces the three principles. Post 3: 'The Customer Proof Angle' -- leads with CFO quote and focuses on Meridian and Strata customer stories. Three genuinely distinct angles with different hooks.

expectations.text: Campaign ROI: The 5 ad copy variants test meaningfully different hooks (e.g., pain-point stat, framework name, customer result, counterintuitive claim, question) with each variant implicitly or explicitly testable as an A/B pair -- without distinct angles, the marketing team is spending ad budget on creative rotation that generates no learning about what resonates with the ICP
expectations.evidence: Variant 1: Pain-Point Stat Hook (11.4 hours/week). Variant 2: Framework Name Hook ('Shift-Left Data Quality'). Variant 3: Customer Result Hook (Meridian 6h to 8min). Variant 4: Counterintuitive Claim Hook ('Your dbt tests pass. Your data is still wrong.'). Variant 5: Question Hook ('When was the last time a non-technical stakeholder found a data bug...'). Five meaningfully different hooks enabling A/B testing of distinct approaches.

expectations.text: Campaign ROI: The 8 email subject line variants comply with the 50-character mobile display limit and span at least 3 of the 5 copywriting frameworks (Specificity, Curiosity, Relevance, Urgency, Question) -- subject lines that exceed mobile character limits get truncated and lose their hook, and testing within a single framework produces marginal gains versus testing across frameworks
expectations.evidence: All 8 subject lines are under 50 characters. Frameworks labeled: (1) Specificity, (2) Curiosity, (3) Relevance, (4) Curiosity, (5) Question, (6) Specificity, (7) Urgency, (8) Relevance. At least 5 frameworks represented: Specificity (2), Curiosity (2), Relevance (2), Question (1), Urgency (1).

expectations.text: Pipeline contribution: The cold email hook is approximately 50 words, leads with a single specific stat from the whitepaper as a conversation opener, and the sales one-pager follows the skill-defined structure (outcome-focused headline, problem bullets, proof with quantified customer results, how-it-works, CTA) -- these two assets are the direct sales-enablement outputs that feed pipeline; generic or unstructured versions get ignored by reps
expectations.evidence: Cold email hook is ~50 words, leads with '11.4 hours per week investigating data freshness issues' stat and includes Meridian proof point. Sales one-pager follows structure: outcome-focused headline ('Stop Discovering Pipeline Breakages from Slack Messages'), problem bullets (3 pain points), proof section with Meridian and Strata results, how-it-works (3 steps: Connect, Map, Detect), and CTA ('Book a 30-minute migration risk assessment').

expectations.text: Brand consistency: All statistics used across every derivative asset are drawn only from the whitepaper brief provided (11.4 hours/week, Meridian 6h to 8min, Strata 100% correction elimination, 200 data teams surveyed) -- fabricated numbers in any single asset create a factual inconsistency across the content ecosystem that erodes brand credibility when a prospect encounters multiple touchpoints
expectations.evidence: Reviewed all 12 assets. Statistics used consistently: 11.4 hours/week (appears in article, posts 1 and 2, newsletter, carousel, cold email, one-pager, ad variants, webinar outline), Meridian 6h to 8min (appears across multiple assets), Strata 100% correction elimination (posts 3, newsletter, carousel, webinar outline), 200 data teams surveyed (article, post 1, carousel, FAQ). No fabricated statistics found.

expectations.text: Production efficiency: Each asset follows its format-specific structure from the skill definition (e.g., LinkedIn article has scroll-stopping first line and ends with a comment-driving question; email newsletter has subject line, preview text, conversational opening, and single CTA; social carousel has 8 slides with one finding per slide) and every asset includes a CTA -- assets that require structural rework before publishing negate the efficiency gain of automated content multiplication
expectations.evidence: LinkedIn article: scroll-stopping first line ('Your Data Team Wastes 11.4 Hours Every Week...') and ends with comment-driving question. Email newsletter: has subject line, preview text, conversational opening ('Hey,'), and single CTA. Carousel: 8 slides with one finding per slide. All assets include CTAs (whitepaper link, assessment booking, or comment prompt). Webinar outline has full agenda structure. FAQ has Q&A format with whitepaper download link.
